rhythms.workday_window: Mon–Fri: core 08:30–12:30 and 14:00–18:30 (peak focus 10:00–16:00). Regular spillover into early evening 17:30–20:30, plus a frequent late pass 21:30–23:00 for mail/web. Lunch dip ~13:00–14:00.
rhythms.leisure_window: Evenings 20:30–23:30 (browsing, messaging, media). Weekends: late start ~10:30/11:00 with activity concentrated 15:00–21:00. Occasional midnight media at ~00:00.
notable_quirks: Daily 00:00 burst of shell commands (~1000) and file access (~700) — looks like scheduled jobs/backups, not interactive work.
Calendar events cluster at 01:00–02:00 local — consistent with all‑day items stored in UTC; not actual night meetings.
Late-evening triage: mail/web and messages spike around 22:00; activity often continues to ~23:00.
Clear lunch lull around 13:00 (screen time and app focus dip), often accompanied by media playback 11:00–14:00.
Friday stands out for heavy terminal use (shell_command >> other days) — likely release/maintenance day.
Afternoon pattern: brief dip ~16:00, rebound at ~17:00 (web activity).
Focus/Do Not Disturb likely toggles around 23:00 (user_focus peak at 23).
Photos activity is extremely high across hours/days, likely background sync/indexing noise rather than active use.
Midweek (especially Wed–Thu) is the busiest; weekends show a sharp drop in web/screen time but mail still checked.
rhythms.summary: Weekdays follow an office-like cadence in Europe/Rome: start ~08:30, strongest work 10:00–16:00, lunch around 13:00, then tapering but often continuing into early evening, with a frequent 22:00–23:00 catch‑up. Weekends are lighter, skewing to afternoon/evening personal use. Nighttime is mostly quiet except for automated jobs at midnight and occasional media at 00:00.

sequences.label: chat + quick settings
sequences.rationale: Rapid back-and-forth between WhatsApp and Control Center suggests adjusting audio/DND while chatting; could be streamlined with keyboard shortcuts, per-app Focus, or a workspace.

sequences.label: terminal + web docs
sequences.rationale: Ping-pong between Terminal and Safari implies running commands while checking docs/search; split view or a dedicated coding workspace would reduce context switches.

sequences.label: web docs + terminal
sequences.rationale: Back-and-forth reference between browser and shell; suggests side-by-side layout or automation to focus the right pane instead of app hopping.

sequences.label: intensive coding with docs
sequences.rationale: Repeated A↔B toggling indicates inefficient context switching; a tiling workspace and hotkeys would help.

sequences.label: share/check location for chat
sequences.rationale: Switching WhatsApp ↔ Find My suggests looking up a location to message; share sheets or side-by-side would reduce toggles.

sequences.label: terminal + web docs (loop)
sequences.rationale: High-frequency alternating between shell and browser; create a coding workspace to avoid repeated app swaps.

sequences.label: quick settings during chat
sequences.rationale: Toggling Control Center around WhatsApp implies adjusting audio/network; map to hardware keys or automate per-app settings.

sequences.label: payment details for chat
sequences.rationale: Likely checking Revolut details and pasting into WhatsApp; use share/copy automation or a split workspace.

sequences.label: capture/share photo for chat
sequences.rationale: Switch to Camera then back to WhatsApp suggests taking a photo or scanning; consider in-app capture or a shortcut.

sequences.label: extended chat + settings toggles
sequences.rationale: Repeated WhatsApp ↔ Control Center cycling indicates ongoing adjustments; Focus automations or keyboard control could help.

sequences.label: coding with docs (heavy)
sequences.rationale: Multi-step ping-pong is a classic docs↔run loop; tile windows and bind focus switching to reduce friction.

sequences.label: gaming + quick settings
sequences.rationale: Adjusting Control Center while gaming (volume/DND) could be automated with per-app Focus or game-specific profiles.

sequences.label: docs ↔ terminal (heavy)
sequences.rationale: Frequent alternation suggests inefficiency; use split view, tmux + browser, or a coding workspace.

sequences.label: connect headphones for chat/call
sequences.rationale: Headphone proximity service appearing around WhatsApp likely indicates device switching; automate preferred audio route.

sequences.label: startup to work
sequences.rationale: Post-login launch of Safari then Terminal; could auto-open a workspace with both apps and layouts.

sequences.label: research with AI assistant
sequences.rationale: Back-and-forth between browser and Claude suggests asking AI about pages; a side-by-side workspace reduces switching.

sequences.label: chat with repeated settings changes
sequences.rationale: Cycle implies ongoing audio/network tweaks; bind to hotkeys or automate per-app.

sequences.label: AI-assisted coding flow
sequences.rationale: Linear progression (ask AI → run in Terminal → check docs) looks like a normal workflow, not inefficient toggling.

sequences.label: music + audio tweak
sequences.rationale: Adjusting Control Center while in Music; media keys or volume automation can avoid app switching.

sequences.label: location share via chat
sequences.rationale: Find My ↔ WhatsApp back-and-forth indicates sharing a location; use share sheet or side-by-side.

sequences.label: cross-posting between messengers
sequences.rationale: Switching WhatsApp ↔ Telegram suggests copying content; consider a unified messenger client or clipboard automation.

sequences.label: terminal + AI assistant
sequences.rationale: Ping-pong to ask AI then return to shell; keep both visible or script prompts via CLI to reduce app flips.

sequences.label: prep settings for gaming
sequences.rationale: Control Center used around game; automate DND/brightness/audio on app launch.

sequences.label: gaming with repeated settings toggles
sequences.rationale: Multiple toggles indicate inefficiency; set Focus or audio profiles to auto-switch.

sequences.label: chat ↔ location lookup (repeated)
sequences.rationale: Prolonged back-and-forth while sharing a location; enable direct share or keep both apps tiled.

sequences.label: share directions via chat
sequences.rationale: WhatsApp ↔ Google Maps suggests copying addresses/links; use share links or side-by-side.

sequences.label: chat during gaming
sequences.rationale: Coordinating in WhatsApp while gaming; in-game chat/overlay or tiled layout would reduce switching.

sequences.label: research → run → ask AI
sequences.rationale: One-way progression across tools; not a repetitive toggle pattern.

sequences.label: cross-posting (Telegram ↔ WhatsApp)
sequences.rationale: Back-and-forth between messengers; clipboard templates or multi-account client could help.

sequences.label: payment details ↔ chat
sequences.rationale: Likely copying Revolut info into WhatsApp; use share/clipboard automation or tile apps.

work_modes.name: Development & AI
work_modes.description: Coding, running commands, researching, and using AI side-by-side. Strong co-activity among Terminal, Safari, Finder, Claude, and Preview.

work_modes.name: Communication & Quick Share
work_modes.description: Messaging and sending media or links. WhatsApp frequently co-appears with Control Center, Camera, Safari, and sometimes Preview for attachments.

work_modes.name: Reading & Reference
work_modes.description: Focused reading of web pages and PDFs with files nearby; Music often plays in the background.

work_modes.name: Errands & Logistics
work_modes.description: Finances, wallet, location checks, and coordinating plans. These apps commonly appear together within the same session.

work_modes.name: Leisure & Media
work_modes.description: Music listening, casual gaming, and light browsing/chatting. Frequent co-activity between Music, WhatsApp, Control Center, Supercell, and Safari.

projects.name: Daily workspace and file management
projects.rationale: Very high and frequent activity (home 730, Downloads 537, Desktop 103) with last use 3–6 days ago indicates ongoing daily work.

projects.name: Powairx website and assets
projects.rationale: Moderate touches (6–7 each) but no activity for 138–187 days.

projects.name: BetterDock (iOS + router)
projects.rationale: Total ~15 touches with last access 164 days ago.

projects.name: Smart-IGCSE platform
projects.rationale: Recent activity (5 days ago) with low volume (4 touches) suggests initial exploration.

projects.name: GoPro media import
projects.rationale: 83 touches on a single day; no activity since (266 days).

projects.name: Obsidian knowledge base
projects.rationale: Occasional updates (8 touches) with moderate recency (49 days) indicate maintenance/organizing rather than active build.

projects.name: Chat prototype
projects.rationale: 6 touches clustered over two days; inactive for 82 days.

projects.name: Checkout prototype
projects.rationale: 4 touches on one day; inactive for 93 days.

browsing.style: Reference-heavy student–developer who bounces between coding tools and school resources, punctuated by social/entertainment bursts. Heavy use of Google as a hub, frequent dev dashboards and AI assistance, and split comms via Gmail/Outlook/Snapchat. Likely a rapid context-switcher with moderate-to-high tab persistence.

idiosyncrasies.description: Peak focus sits 10:00–16:00 with intense Terminal ↔ Safari back‑and‑forth during coding/research.
Fridays show outsized Terminal activity (deploy/build/railway workflows) — likely a release/maintenance cadence.
A large automated burst of shell commands and file opens fires right at 00:00 (backups/jobs), with little/no user input — not real usage.
Late‑evening triage window 21:30–23:00: Gmail-in-Safari + WhatsApp spike; Focus/DND often toggled near 23:00.
Clear lunch lull around 13:00 with Music playing between ~11:00–14:00.
Frequent WhatsApp ⇄ Control Center toggles (audio/DND adjustments), often alongside headphone proximity events.
WhatsApp is frequently paired with Find My (location lookup/share) and Camera (quick capture → share).
Photos background indexing is noisy across hours/days and inflates perceived “Photos usage.”
Weekday login pattern quickly flows into Safari then Terminal (startup-to-work ramp).
